summary:
Balkan Gypsy Jazz from Barcelona

Bratia is a Barcelona band formed by musicians with international careers — Ivan Kovačević, Pere Nolasc Plana, Victor Paradis, Julien Chanal and Stelios Togias — specialising in the fusion of manouche jazz, Balkan music and gypsy jazz.

The previous website was a heavy, hard-to-maintain WordPress that required technical intervention for any change. The real problem: touring musicians don’t have time to manage websites.

The problem

An active band needs the website to update itself.
title: Bratia

summary:
A Japanese restaurant in the heart of Barcelona

Machiroku is a small family restaurant specialising in authentic Japanese cuisine in the heart of Barcelona.

The previous website was a conventional WordPress: functional, but heavy, hard to maintain and with no adaptation to the restaurant’s real needs.

The problem

A restaurant needs to update its menu, opening hours and notices frequently. With WordPress, every change required accessing a complex manager or depending on someone external.
title: Machiroku

summary:
A cultural space that deserves a tool built for it

Nau Bostik is a self-managed sociocultural space in the Sagrera neighbourhood, Barcelona. Intense activity, diverse community, constant agenda and completely horizontal management.

The current website works, but it doesn’t accompany. It’s a showcase, not a tool.

The vision

We are developing a proposal to turn Nau Bostik’s website into a real tool for management, communication and governance of the organisation.
title: Nau Bostik

summary:
An independent photography publisher

112Books is an independent publisher focused on photobooks and photographic projects.

The challenge was not just publishing content. It was structuring a coherent and sustainable digital editorial system.

Website currently under renovation — the new version will include a news blog and an integrated shop, built with Hugo to reduce weight and improve performance.

→ 112books.eu
title: 112Books

summary:
A platform for new voices in photography

112Revelats is a project by publisher 112Books to give visibility to emerging photographers.

Two annual open calls where new authors can publish a digital photobook in a collective format. If the project grows, also in print.

Current website in WordPress, in the process of migration to Hugo now that the editorial model is well defined.

→ 112revelats.112books.eu
title: 112Revelats

summary:
Technology consultancy by the hour

MalditasMaquinas is a technology consultancy service designed for those who need occasional advice without long contracts or commitments.

The model is simple: buy consultancy hours and ask questions by email. The status of remaining hours and the full consultation history always accessible from a private area within the website.

A way to give professional responses to the regular queries that arrive by phone and are hard to invoice.
title: MalditasMaquinas

summary:
The Catalan neighbourhood movement’s magazine online

Carrer is the publication of FAVB — the Federation of Neighbourhood Associations of Barcelona — with decades of experience defending neighbourhood rights.

The challenge was to bring a consolidated print publication to the internet without losing its editorial essence.

The solution

We built the project from scratch with two clear axes:

Complete digital archive All magazine issues in PDF, accessible, organised and searchable. Decades of neighbourhood journalism, available online.
title: Carrer.cat

summary:
A photography agency with its own character

Pocallum is a Barcelona photography agency with a very defined identity and an unconventional way of working.

Current website in WordPress, under review. A close project where technology and photographic vision must go completely hand in hand.

→ pocallum.cat
title: Pocallum

summary:
This very website

LinuxBCN.com is itself an example of the system we propose to our clients.

Built with Hugo, lightweight, fast and maintainable. Trilingual from day one. No unnecessary plugins, no external dependencies.

The methodology we apply to every project applied first to ourselves.

Before and after

The previous website: accumulative WordPress, hard to maintain.

The new system: lightweight, structured and coherent.

→ linuxbcn.com → How we work
title: LinuxBCN

summary:
The usual situation

You have a website, social media, content — but no system.

Concerts not updated. Biography different on every platform. Videos on YouTube, photos on Instagram, music on Spotify, and the website connects none of it.

Any change requires touching five different places. Or depending on someone else to do it.

What we propose

A digital presence designed as a system.

A website that connects what already exists — concerts, music, videos, photographs — without duplicating content or creating new dependencies.
title: Solutions for artists and musicians

summary:
The usual situation

The collective works. The digital communication doesn’t.

The website is hard to update — it always depends on someone. Meetings are called via WhatsApp. Documents live on Google Drive without anyone having decided that. Photos are on someone’s Instagram who may no longer be in the collective.

It’s not bad will. It’s that nobody designed the system.

What we propose

Organising the collective’s digital ecosystem with criteria.
title: Solutions for cultural entities and collectives

summary:
The usual situation

The business works. The digital presence doesn’t quite work.

The website was made by someone years ago and nobody knows how to touch it. You’re on Instagram because “you have to be” but it generates nothing. You pay subscriptions to tools you don’t remember why you signed up for. Every update requires calling someone or waiting until they have time.

The problem isn’t the technology. It’s that nobody thought the system through from the start.
title: Solutions for values-driven businesses
